# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This website hosts the materials for the **FloodTraces** policy workshop and hackathon:

> *Integrating Digital and Traditional Data for Real-Time Humanitarian Decision-Making*

Led by the [Geographic Data Science Lab](https://www.liverpool.ac.uk/geographic-data-science/) at the **University of Liverpool**, in partnership with the [Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO)](https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/foreign-commonwealth-development-office) and the [International Organization for Migration's Displacement Tracking Matrix (DTM)](https://dtm.iom.int/).

**FloodTraces** develops a replicable data pipeline combining **GPS mobility data**, social media signals, satellite imagery, and traditional survey sources to produce timely, spatially detailed estimates of flood-driven population displacement. The pipeline generates **Digital Traces Situation Reports (SitReps)** — concise, policy-oriented documents designed for humanitarian decision-makers in the critical first weeks after a disaster.

::: {style="text-align: center; margin: 2rem 0;"}
<img src="img/floodtraces_logo.svg" width="680" alt="FloodTraces logo: a point displaced by flooding and returning to origin"/>
:::

---

## Beyond Floods {.unnumbered}

While the project's focus is flood-related displacement, the Digital Traces methodology is designed to transfer across disaster contexts. As a demonstration of this, we have applied the same GPS pipeline approach to **conflict-induced displacement** — producing a SitRep for Iran in early 2026:

> [**Situation Report — Iran Conflict: Digital Trace Data Analysis (28 Feb – 19 Mar 2026)**](https://reliefweb.int/report/iran-islamic-republic/situation-report-iran-conflict-digital-trace-data-analysis-experimental-reporting-period-28th-february-2026-19th-march-2026)  
> *Experimental reporting. Published on ReliefWeb.*

This report illustrates how the same analytical approach — analysing population movement signals from digital trace data against a baseline period — can be adapted rapidly to new geographies and crisis types, with appropriate caveats on data coverage and representativeness.

---

## Event Overview {.unnumbered}

The **22–24 June 2026** event at the London School of Economic (LSE) combines a policy workshop and a two-day hackathon. Click any row for details.

| Day | Format | Audience | Focus |
|---|---|---|---|
| [**Day 1 — 22 June**](workshop.qmd) | Policy Workshop | Senior policy, FCDO, IOM, NGOs + hackathon participants | Indonesia & Colombia SitRep presentations; structured feedback; methods clinic; data walkthrough |
| [**Days 2–3 — 23–24 June**](workshop.qmd) | Hackathon | Technical teams + returning policy stakeholders (Day 3 afternoon) | Pakistan case study: three problem statements across data, interaction, and anticipatory action |

---

## Join Us {.unnumbered}

**Day 1 of the policy workshop (22 June) is open to remote attendance.** If you work in humanitarian response, policy, or displacement data and would like to join online, we welcome your participation.

To register interest or find out more, contact **Elisabetta Pietrostefani** at [e.pietrostefani@liverpool.ac.uk](mailto:e.pietrostefani@liverpool.ac.uk).

The hackathon (Days 2–3) is for pre-registered participants only. If you are interested in future editions, please get in touch.

---

## Case Studies at a Glance {.unnumbered}

| Country | Event | Analysis Window | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| [**Indonesia**](researchareas.qmd) | West Sumatra floods & landslides | Feb–Mar 2024 | SitRep — presented Day 1 |
| [**Colombia**](researchareas.qmd) | Floods & conflict | Oct–Nov 2024 | SitRep — presented Day 1 |
| [**Pakistan**](researchareas.qmd) | Monsoon floods | Aug–Oct 2022 | Hackathon case study |
| [**Iran**](https://reliefweb.int/report/iran-islamic-republic/situation-report-iran-conflict-digital-trace-data-analysis-experimental-reporting-period-28th-february-2026-19th-march-2026) | Conflict displacement | Feb–Mar 2026 | Example SitRep — wider methodology |

---

## Contact {.unnumbered}

> **Dr Elisabetta Pietrostefani** — e.pietrostefani \[at\] liverpool.ac.uk  

> **Dr Martina Pardy** — m.l.pardy \[at\] liverpool.ac.uk

> **Matt Mason** — matt.mason \[at\] liverpool.ac.uk

> **Andrea Nasuto** — andrea.nasuto \[at\] liverpool.ac.uk

> **Prof Francisco Rowe** — fcorowe \[at\] liverpool.ac.uk

The website is licensed under the [Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/).
